description: fourth-highest-ranking official in the United States Department of Justice
description_wiki: The solicitor general of the United States (USSG or SG), is an official in the United States Department of Justice who represents the federal government in cases before the Supreme Court of the United States.

description: president of the United States from 1869 to 1877
description_wiki: Ulysses S. Grant (born Hiram Ulysses Grant; April 27, 1822 – July 23, 1885) was the 18th president of the United States, serving from 1869 to 1877.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: hospital in California, United States
building in Alabama, United States
description_wiki: The Daniel Building is a 20-story, 238 foot (86 m) office building located in downtown Birmingham, Alabama.

description: 1950 film by Stuart Heisler
description_wiki: Tulsa is a 1949 American Western action film directed by Stuart Heisler and starring Susan Hayward and Robert Preston, and featured Lloyd Gough, Chill Wills (as the narrator), and Ed Begley in one of his earliest film roles, billed as Edward Begley.

description: American actress (1917–1975)
description_wiki: Susan Hayward (born Edythe Marrener; June 30, 1917 – March 14, 1975) was an American actress best known for her film portrayals of women that were based on true stories.

description: American actor, vocalist (1900-1970)
description_wiki: Preston Stratton Foster (August 24, 1900 – July 14, 1970), was an American actor of stage, film, radio, and television, whose career spanned nearly four decades.

description: city in Miami-Dade County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Miami is a coastal city in the U.S. state of Florida.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: mental and behavioral disorder due to use of alcohol: withdrawal state with delirium (disorder)
description: rapid onset of confusion caused by alcohol withdrawal
description_wiki: Delirium tremens (DTs; lit. 'mental disturbance with shaking') is a rapid onset of confusion caused by withdrawal from alcohol.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

surface_forms: Douglas was the bomber plant at the airport in Tulsa
description: 1921-1967 aerospace manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: The Douglas Aircraft Company was an American aerospace and defense company that was based in Southern California.

description: U.S. government agency responsible for mortgage insurance
description_wiki: The Federal Housing Administration (FHA), also known as the Office of Housing within the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), is a United States government agency founded by President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, created in part by the National Housing Act of 1934.

description: Attorney and judge (1904-1975)
description_wiki: Alfred Paul Murrah (October 27, 1904 – October 30, 1975) was a United States circuit judge of the United States Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit and previously was a United States district judge of the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Oklahoma, the United States District Court for the Northern District of Oklahoma and the United States District Court for the Western District of Oklahoma.

description: former federal building that was bombed by Timothy McVeigh
description_wiki: The Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building was a United States federal government complex located at 200 N.W. 5th Street in downtown Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.

description: 1995 terrorist attack in the United States
description_wiki: On April 19, 1995, American anti-government extremist Timothy McVeigh, assisted by Terry Nichols, detonated a makeshift bomb stored in a rental truck parked in front of the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, in an act of domestic terrorism.

entity: United States Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit
surface_forms: United States Circuit Court of Appeals
Chief Justice of the United States Circuit Court of Appeals
description: current United States federal appellate court
description_wiki:
The United States Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit (in case citations, 10th Cir.) is a federal court with appellate jurisdiction over the district courts in the following districts:

District of Colorado
District of Kansas
District of New Mexico
Eastern District of Oklahoma
Northern District of Oklahoma
Western District of Oklahoma
District of Utah
District of Wyoming
Although the 37th United States Congress passed the Tenth Circuit Act of 1863, the circuit ceased to exist after six years due to the Judiciary Act of 1869.
